chains.name: Tech

signals.note: Slightly above average, within normal range

signals.source: USPTO patent filings by tracked companies (24h)

chains.conclusion: The Tech chain shows no RISING signals today. Hacker News engagement with keywords including autonomous driving, protein folding, and quantum computing has dropped sharply — 25% below the 30-day average — suggesting reduced community interest or a weekend/holiday effect suppressing discourse. Crunchbase tech funding news is also at zero versus a 1.27 average, indicating a dry day for deal announcements in AI and autonomous systems. arXiv output is essentially flat. No patent filings were recorded. Overall, the chain is in a quiet, slightly negative posture.

chains.name: Economy

signals.source: Sector layoff news items — tracked industries (24h Google News count)
Sector job posting volume — Adzuna (sum across categories)

chains.conclusion: The Economy chain shows two DROPPING signals and one SILENT. The most striking reading is Adzuna job posting volume collapsing to 1,281 from a 30-day average of 54,073 — a 98% drop that almost certainly reflects a data pipeline or API anomaly rather than a genuine labor market event, and should be treated with extreme caution. Layoff news is also well below average at 17 items versus 48.8, which is directionally positive but may reflect weekend news suppression. The yield spread is stable. No actionable economic signal today.

chains.name: Biotech

signals.source: NIH Reporter active grants this FY

chains.conclusion: NIH Reporter active grants have risen to 11,960 versus a 30-day average of 10,048, a 19% increase that sits just below the 20% RISING threshold but represents a meaningful acceleration in federal funding deployment — likely reflecting fiscal year grant award cycles benefiting programmes in protein folding, CRISPR, mRNA, and GLP-1 adjacent research. bioRxiv preprint volume is modestly below average. ClinicalTrials enrollment is flat. S-1 biotech IPO filings are absent today, consistent with a cautious capital markets environment. The NIH signal is the sole notable mover; convergence is insufficient to flag.

chains.name: Social

signals.source: Google Trends avg interest score (7d window)

chains.conclusion: Google Trends interest in monitored keywords — autonomous vehicle, robotaxi, protein folding, and small modular reactor — has dropped 43% below the 30-day average, signalling a meaningful pullback in public search engagement with these themes. This may reflect news cycle rotation away from AI and clean energy topics, or a weekend suppression effect. Bluesky data is silent. Kickstarter and Amazon signals are flat. The Social chain is in a quiet, slightly negative posture with no actionable convergence.

chains.name: Geopolitics

signals.source: % of tracked commodities above own 30d baseline

chains.conclusion: The commodity breadth indicator — measuring the percentage of tracked commodities (lithium, copper, wheat, crude oil, gold) above their 30-day baselines — has collapsed to 80 from a 973.6 average. This reading is almost certainly a data normalisation or scaling anomaly rather than a genuine signal that all five commodities have simultaneously fallen below baseline. Shipping congestion, EU consultations, and Congressional hearings are all flat at zero. No geopolitical signal is actionable today; the commodity metric requires data quality verification before interpretation.

chains.name: Corporate

signals.source: SEC Form 4 insider filings for watchlist (24h)
Keyword-matched job postings across watchlist companies (total)
USPTO patent filings by watchlist companies (24h)

chains.conclusion: Corporate watchlist job postings have risen to 13 versus a 10.8 average, just crossing the RISING threshold at +20.4%. This is driven by steady hiring at Recursion Pharmaceuticals (4 postings), Zscaler (4), Beam Therapeutics (2), WeRide (2), and Arvinas (1) — all at or near their individual baselines, suggesting broad-based stability rather than a single company surge. SEC Form 4 insider filings and 8-K material event filings are both at zero today, a notable quiet period for the watchlist that may reflect weekend filing patterns. No strong corporate signal today.

chains.name: Energy

signals.source: Average energy commodity price (lithium, uranium, copper)

chains.conclusion: The average price of tracked energy commodities — lithium, uranium, and copper — has risen to 3,200 versus a 30-day average of 2,988, a 7.1% gain that does not clear the 20% RISING threshold but represents a directionally positive move for battery materials and nuclear fuel inputs. This is consistent with ongoing demand for EV supply chain materials and uranium driven by small modular reactor programme momentum. ARPA-E data is silent. arXiv applied physics output is flat. Energy funding news is absent. Convergence is insufficient to flag a signal.
